heading: Assistance for providers of transportation services affected by COVID–19
label: 9111 Assistance for providers of transportation services affected by COVID–19

children.label: A
children.text: beginning on ; andDecember 27, 2020

children.label: i
children.text: ; orMarch 31, 2021

children.label: ii
children.text: the date on which all funds provided to the provider of transportation services under subsection (c) are expended.

children.heading: COVID–19
children.label: 2 COVID–19
children.text: The term “COVID–19” means the Coronavirus Disease 2019.

children.label: I
children.text: salary, wage, commission, or similar compensation;

children.label: II
children.text: payment of a cash tip or an equivalent;

children.label: III
children.text: payment for vacation, parental, family, medical, or sick leave;

children.label: IV
children.text: payment required for the provision of group health care or other group insurance benefits, including insurance premiums;

children.label: V
children.text: payment of a retirement benefit;

children.label: VI
children.text: payment of a State or local tax assessed on employees with respect to compensation; or

children.label: VII
children.text: paid administrative leave; and

children.label: aa
children.text: a wage;

children.label: bb
children.text: a commission;

children.label: cc
children.text: income;

children.label: dd
children.text: net earnings from self-employment; or

children.label: ee
children.text: similar compensation; and

children.label: II
children.text: in an amount equal to not more than $100,000 during 1 calendar year, as prorated for the covered period.

children.label: i
children.text: any compensation of an individual employee in excess of an annual salary of $100,000, as prorated for the covered period;

children.label: ii
children.text: any tax imposed or withheld under chapter 21, 22, or 24 of title 26 during the covered period;

children.label: iii
children.text: any compensation of an employee whose principal place of residence is outside the United States;

children.label: iv
children.text: any qualified sick leave wages for which a credit is allowed under section 7001 of the Families First Coronavirus Response Act ( note; );26 U.S.C. 3111Public Law 116–127

children.label: v
children.text: any qualified family leave wages for which a credit is allowed under section 7003 of that Act ( note; ); or26 U.S.C. 3111Public Law 116–127

children.label: vi
children.text: any bonus, raise in excess of inflation, or other form of additional employee compensation.

children.label: i
children.text: in the United States; or

children.label: ii
children.text: pursuant to Federal law;

children.label: B
children.text: has significant operations, and a majority of employees based, in the United States;

children.label: C
children.text: was in operation on ; andMarch 1, 2020

children.label: I
children.text: a passenger vessel (as defined in section 2101 of that title) carrying fewer than 2,400 passengers;

children.label: II
children.text: a small passenger vessel (as defined in section 2101 of that title); or

children.label: III
children.text: a vessel providing pilotage services and regulated by a State in accordance with chapter 85 of that title;

children.label: ii
children.text: a company providing transportation services using a bus characterized by an elevated passenger deck located over a baggage compartment (commonly known as an “over-the-road bus”), including local and intercity fixed-route service, commuter service, and charter or tour service (including tour or excursion service that includes features in addition to bus transportation, such as meals, lodging, admission to points of interest or special attractions, or the services of a guide);

children.label: iii
children.text: a company providing transportation services using a school bus (as defined in section 571.3 of title 49, Code of Federal Regulations (or successor regulations)); or

children.label: iv
children.text: any other passenger transportation service company subject to regulation by the Department of Transportation as the Secretary, in consultation with the Secretary of Transportation, determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 5 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: b Funding
children.text: Out of any funds in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, there are appropriated to provide grants to eligible providers of transportation services under this section, $2,000,000,000 for fiscal year 2021, to remain available until expended.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary, in consultation with the Secretary of Transportation, shall use the amounts made available under subsection (b) to provide grants to eligible providers of transportation services described in paragraph (2) that certify to the Secretary that the providers of transportation services have experienced a revenue loss of 25 percent or more, on an annual basis, as a direct or indirect result of COVID–19.

children.heading: Description of eligible providers of transportation services
children.label: 2 Description of eligible providers of transportation services
I
children.text: had 500 or fewer full-time, part-time, or temporary employees; and

children.label: II
children.text: was not a subsidiary, parent, or affiliate of any other entity with a combined total workforce of more than 500 full-time, part-time, or temporary employees; or

children.label: I
children.text: on , had more than 500 full-time, part-time, or temporary employees; andMarch 1, 2020

children.label: II
children.text: has not received assistance under paragraph (1), (2), or (3) of , or subtitle B of title IV of division A, of the Coronavirus Aid, Relief, and Economic Security Act (; ) [ et seq.].section 9042(b) of this title1Public Law 116–136134 Stat. 28115 U.S.C. 9071So in original. The comma probably should not appear.1

children.heading: Scope of eligibility for certain companies
children.label: B Scope of eligibility for certain companies

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A provider of transportation services that has entered into or maintains a contract or agreement described in clause (ii) shall not be determined to be ineligible for assistance under this subsection on the basis of that contract or agreement, subject to clause (iv).

children.heading: Contract or agreement described
children.label: ii Contract or agreement described
children.text: A contract or agreement referred to in clause (i) is a contract or agreement for transportation services that is supported by a public entity using funds received under the Emergency Appropriations for Coronavirus Health Response and Agency Operations (division B of ; ).Public Law 116–136134 Stat. 505

children.heading: Adjustment of assistance
children.label: iii Adjustment of assistance
children.text: The Secretary may reduce the amount of assistance available under this subsection to a provider of transportation services described in clause (i) based on the amount of funds provided under this section or the Emergency Appropriations for Coronavirus Health Response and Agency Operations (division B of ; ) that have supported a contract or agreement described in clause (ii) to which the provider of transportation services is a party.Public Law 116–136134 Stat. 505

children.heading: Notice requirement
children.label: iv Notice requirement
children.text: A provider of transportation services that has entered into or maintains a contract or agreement described in clause (ii), and that applies for assistance under this subsection, shall submit to the Secretary a notice describing the contract or agreement, including the amount of funds provided for the contract or agreement under this subsection or the Emergency Appropriations for Coronavirus Health Response and Agency Operations (division B of ; ).Public Law 116–136134 Stat. 505

children.label: i
children.text: the amount of debt owed by the provider of transportation services on major equipment, if any;

children.label: ii
children.text: other sources of Federal assistance provided to the provider of transportation services, if any; and

children.label: iii
children.text: such other information as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Award
children.label: i Award
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that the amount of assistance provided to a provider of transportation services under this subsection, when combined with any other Federal assistance provided in response to COVID–19 under the Coronavirus Aid, Relief, and Economic Security Act (; ), the Paycheck Protection Program and Health Care Enhancement Act (; ), or any other provision of law, does not exceed the total amount of revenue earned by the provider of transportation services during calendar year 2019.Public Law 116–136134 Stat. 281Public Law 116–139134 Stat. 620

children.label: I
children.text: documentation describing the total amount of revenue earned by the provider of transportation services during calendar year 2019; and

children.label: II
children.text: a certification that the amount of assistance sought under this subsection, when combined with any other Federal assistance described in clause (i), does not exceed the total amount of revenue earned by the provider of transportation services during calendar year 2019.

children.heading: Form of assistance
children.label: 4 Form of assistance
children.text: The amounts made available under subsection (b) shall be provided to eligible providers of transportation services in the form of grants.

children.heading: Equal access
children.label: 5 Equal access
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure equal access to the assistance provided under this section to eligible providers of transportation services that are small, minority-owned, and women-owned businesses.

children.label: I
children.text: retirement; or

children.label: II
children.text: voluntary employee separation;

children.label: I
children.text: any involuntary furlough; or

children.label: II
children.text: any reduction in pay rates or benefits for nonexecutive employees; and

children.label: iii
children.text: to recall or rehire any employees laid off, furloughed, or terminated after , to the extent warranted by increased service levels;March 27, 2020

children.label: B
children.text: to return to the Secretary any funds received under this subsection that are not used by the provider of transportation services by the date that is 1 year after the date of receipt of the funds; and

children.label: C
children.text: to examine the anticipated expenditure of the funds by the provider of transportation services for the purposes described in subparagraph (A) not less frequently than once every 90 days after the date of receipt of the funds.

children.label: A
children.text: the date that is 30 days after the date of receipt of the funds; and

children.label: B
children.text: the date that is 90 days after .December 27, 2020

children.heading: Prioritization of payroll costs
children.label: A Prioritization of payroll costs
children.text: As a condition of receipt of a grant under this subsection, the Secretary shall require that, except as provided in subparagraph (B), a provider of transportation services shall agree to use an amount equal to not less than 60 percent of the funds on payroll costs of the provider of transportation services.

children.label: I
children.text: if laid off, furloughed, or terminated by the provider of transportation services as described in paragraph (6)(A)(iii), is rehired, or has been offered rehire, by the provider of transportation services; and

children.label: II
children.text: if rehired under clause (i) or subject to a reduction in salary before the date of receipt by the provider of transportation services of assistance under this subsection, receives not less than 100 percent of the previous salary of the employee;

children.label: I
children.text: is staffed at a level of full-time equivalent, seasonal employees, on a monthly basis, that is greater than or equivalent to the level at which the provider of transportation services was staffed with full-time equivalent, seasonal employees on a monthly basis during calendar year 2019;

children.label: II
children.text: is offering priority in rehiring to seasonal employees that were laid off, furloughed, terminated, or not offered rehire in calendar year 2020, as the provider of transportation services achieves staffing at the level described in subclause (I); and

children.label: III
children.text: offers any seasonal employee rehired under subclause (II) or subject to a reduction in salary before the date of receipt by the provider of transportation services of assistance under this subsection not less than 100 percent of the previous salary of the employee; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the provider of transportation services will fully cover, through the applicable covered period, all payroll costs associated with the staffing requirements described in clauses (i) and (ii).

children.heading: Forms; terms and conditions
children.label: 9 Forms; terms and conditions
children.text: A grant provided under this section shall be in such form, subject to such terms and conditions, and contain such covenants, representations, warranties, and requirements (including requirements for audits) as the Secretary determines to be appropriate in accordance with this section.

children.label: A
children.text: the payment of payroll costs;

children.label: B
children.text: the acquisition of services, equipment, including personal protective equipment, and other measures needed to protect workers and customers from COVID–19;

children.label: i
children.text: including rent, leases, insurance, and interest on regularly scheduled debt service; but

children.label: ii
children.text: not including any prepayment of, or payment of principal on, a debt obligation, except for any principal on a debt obligation accrued by the provider of transportation services directly to maintain the expenditures of the provider of transportation services on payroll costs throughout the COVID–19 pandemic; or

children.label: D
children.text: the compensation of returning employees for lost pay and benefits during the COVID–19 pandemic, subject to subsection (e).

children.heading: Eligibility
children.label: 2 Eligibility
children.text: The use of assistance provided under subsection (c) for the compensation of returning employees under paragraph (1)(D) shall be counted toward the required amount of grants to be used on payroll costs under subsection (c)(6)(A).

children.label: i
children.text: furlough pay;

children.label: ii
children.text: severance pay; or

children.label: iii
children.text: separation pay; and

children.label: B
children.text: any amounts the employee received from unemployment insurance; and

children.label: A
children.text: be considered an overpayment for purposes of any State or Federal unemployment law; or

children.label: B
children.text: be subject to any overpayment recovery efforts by a State agency (as defined in section 205 of the Federal-State Extended Unemployment Compensation Act of 1970 (U.S.C. 3304 note )).2So in original. Probably should be “ note”.226 U.S.C. 3304

children.label: A
children.text: using direct hiring authority to hire employees to administer this section;

children.label: B
children.text: entering into contracts, including contracts for services authorized by this section; and

children.label: C
children.text: issuing such regulations and other guidance as may be necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of this section.

children.heading: Administrative expenses
children.label: 2 Administrative expenses
children.text: Of the funds made available under this section, not more than $50,000,000 may be used by the Secretary for administrative expenses to carry out this section.

children.heading: Availability for obligation
children.label: 3 Availability for obligation
children.text: The funds made available under this section shall remain available for obligation until the date that is 3 years after .December 27, 2020
